BIG AGENCY THINKING, WITHOUT THE PRICE TAG.
Raising The Steaks, For A Hong Kong Launch.
Raising The Steaks, For A Hong Kong Launch.
BREAKING AND REBUILDING THE AGENCY MODEL.

We are an independent communications agency, established in 2019. We believe it’s time to be better partners for our clients. Our vision is to break, and re-build an out-of- date agency model. We don’t want to talk about our approach to planning communications. We want to understand your business challenges, and figure out how we can help. Our vision is to design lean, agile and effective teams around the specific needs of each individual client. We won’t inflate your costs with high overheads and overpaid senior members of staff.
We are genuine and transparent in everything that we do, and want to achieve transformational results through real partnerships.
BREAKING AND REBUILDING THE AGENCY MODEL.
BREAKING AND REBUILDING THE AGENCY MODEL.
BREAKING AND REBUILDING THE AGENCY MODEL.

We have lean teams that can deliver all digital marketing services for our clients. We allocate the best talent, for the best price, based on what you need. Most of all, we want to get away from the jargon, and acronyms that overcomplicate advertising. You’ll get straight honest answers, and clear business results.
We start by listening, focusing on your challenges, genuinely getting a handle on how marketing can help.
We research – using bespoke access to data and consumer panels, to show you clearly how advertising can deliver growth.
We set up the right strategy and team that will deliver results focused, data-driven advertising; maximising your return.

Charlie has over 15 years of experience, leading global clients in London, Dubai, Hong Kong and Singapore. He was most recently APAC Chief Media Officer for Digitas, an integrated full service agency headquartered in Singapore.​

His most recent key accounts were Samsung, Pan Pacific Hotels, Shangri La Hotels, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, & Visa. During his time at Digitas, he dedicated himself to powering innovation through the smart use of new technologies and data. Key to his success was working closely with creative, data, and technology teams, to create highly personalized and engaging experiences.

Ben has over 10 years’ experience as a Digital media professional, leading both regional and global strategy & activation, across several continents. Throughout his career, he has been responsible for leading campaigns for some of the worlds largest brands, across both Digital and Traditional media.

Initially, he stared as a performance specialist, working at the UKs largest independent performance agencies, where he was the global agency performance lead for Brands such as Nespresso and Mercedes Benz. During this period, he gained a vast experience across multiple verticals, ranging from Travel, Luxury products, Ecommerce, and B2B Tech.

WE’D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU.
WHAT PERSONAL DATA WE COLLECT AND WHY WE COLLECT IT
When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.
If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select “Remember Me”, your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.
WHO WE SHARE YOUR DATA WITH
HOW LONG WE RETAIN YOUR DATA

If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.
WHAT RIGHTS YOU HAVE OVER YOUR DATA

If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.
Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.
WHAT DATA BREACH PROCEDURES WE HAVE IN PLACE
WHAT THIRD PARTIES WE RECEIVE DATA FROM
WHAT AUTOMATED DECISION MAKING AND/OR PROFILING WE DO WITH USER DATA
Technical and content recommendations that will enable your website to appear more prominently on top traffic generating search engines.
Site audit, encompassing the 3 main pillars of SEO.
Technical audit – ensuring engines can access your site correctly; demonstrates TRUST.
On-page Content review – reviewing site content and structure; demonstrates RELEVANCY.
Off-site Signals analysis – analyze the number and quality of signals, pointing to your site from other sites; demonstrates AUTHORITY.
These findings are then combined into a holistic strategy, with clear & realistic KPIs, based on your business objectives.
Improved website visibility within search engines, thus increasing scale and quality of traffic to your website.
Improving the 3 pillars will also have halo-effect on overall brand experience, as partnerships can be defined by outreach activity.
User experience improves by improved content relevancy.

We lead all e-commerce activities for Bacha Coffee. We began our partnership by mining their first party data for audience insights to power digital strategy. We built detailed consumer purchase journeys, and messaging frameworks that track how their products are discovered. This slickened the path to purchase. We also guided their expansion into new markets in Asia and North America. Managing all media channels we’ve driven compelling improvements and returns during key sales periods.

Raising The Steaks, For A Hong Kong Launch.
We launched the Beefsteak Club Wine portfolio in Hong Kong. We dove deep into wine industry and market data, honing the market entry strategy for a brand big in the UK but new to Asia.

We led creative strategy and design work for the launch of all social media channels and built a local website. A compelling content series on local wine and food pairings drove significant engagement and reach with wine and foodies in Hong Kong.

We have worked with Far East on the launch of two luxury condos in Singapore, Ambersand and The Cairnhill. We produced detailed analysis into high-net-worth consumer in Asia. We then developed a clear audience data and messaging strategy, personalizing the needs of the consumer to the attributes of the luxury condos. This campaign enabled us to develop some exciting rich media mobile executions, bringing to life the luxurious interiors of the buildings within ad units.

We were a launch partner for Hugo Save, a new fin tech savings application in Singapore. In the early stages we guided audience targeting strategy, data and measurement. We developed customer acquisition journeys, and mapped messages to optimize the path to becoming a registered customer. We also handled the implementation of some “app acquisition” mobile programmatic test campaigns, evaluating the efficacy of different data, optimization and targeting strategies.

We partnered with Indosole, to help hone their brand strategy and develop more incisive creative work. We began by getting under the bonnet of all their data and media channels. This unlocked some great insights into who was really buying their products, and how they could then hone creative work to engage these audiences in new markets.
After guiding the development of a clearer brand strategy, the output was a campaign that delivered significant improvements in creative impact.

We lead the strategy for, procure, and negotiate McKinsey’s content activations in Asia Pacific for their “Future of Asia” campaign. We produced detailed analysis of C Suite media and digital behaviours, identifying opportunities to improve the way that they connect with business leaders in the region. We created a partnership with Bloomberg, developing a content hub, and disseminating articles throughout their ecosystem with innovative new formats. We have also led training sessions on media and social strategy for their internal teams.

We worked with Muuse to help guide brand, creative and media strategy to help them grow in Asia. Muuse are an up-and-coming re-usable cup business where you can pick up and drop off coffee cups and takeaway containers across the city. We conducted customer research, building detailed profiles and insights to help guide their strategy. We then focused their brand positioning, oversaw the development of a new creative campaign, and managed the media implementation.

We partnered with Nizoral in South Korea and Thailand, to power their brand video strategy. We took their existing videos, ran pre-tests to identify areas for improvement, and then adapted the videos with dynamic video frames to enhance them. We then managed the implementation of scaled programmatic video campaigns across both countries, using data partners to identify high value audiences. The campaigns were highly successful. The media buy delivered low cost per views, and the creative work drove positive brand impact. We also handled Naver Search across South Korea.
